hint.checkEmail: Instructions sent! Check your email and follow the link to reset your password.
hint.checkEmailToConfirm: Account created! Check your email and follow the link to confirm your address before signing in.
hint.resetSuccess: Password reset successfully! Redirecting to login…

error.signupFailed: Could not create your account. The username or email may already be in use.
error.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 6 characters.
error.tokenMissing: Token not found in URL. Request a new recovery link.

welcome.title: Welcome to MCP Convert
welcome.body: This tool connects your existing APIs to AI assistants like Claude Desktop and Cursor — without writing a single line of code. Your AI will be able to search, create, update and retrieve data from any API you connect here.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.step1Body: give it a name and upload your API documentation (OpenAPI / Swagger) or paste the URL. If you don't have the file, try pasting the API base URL and clicking "Discover".
howItWorks.step2Body: add the API credentials (token, key, password). They are stored securely and injected automatically on every request.
howItWorks.step3Body: paste it in your AI client's settings. That's it - your assistant can now use the API.

keepSecure.title: Keep it secure
keepSecure.intro: Your MCP endpoint is accessible to anyone who has the URL. Before sharing it with external clients, we recommend:
keepSecure.step1Body: found in each server under "MCP Authentication". Every client must include it in their configuration.
keepSecure.step2Strong: Use the Share with client button
keepSecure.step2Body: generates step-by-step setup instructions for your clients, with the key already filled in.
keepSecure.step3Body: prevents your API from being overwhelmed.

allSet.title: You're all set!
allSet.body: Start by creating your first server. If you run into trouble, every panel has a help button (?) with detailed explanations. You can always come back to this guide from the Help menu.
